hard.next_step: Vendor selection call on Monday Final commerical pack sent today - Andrew back in office Wednesday - feedback end of week start of next Commercial Feedback with Andrew and Elaine by 18th June Decision pending in June Contract Redlining and Signing July POC was successful - best Platform
hard.competitor: Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal; GEP (declined); Coupa (declined); Proactis (incumbent)

north_star_verdict.headline: Bright Horizons confirmed selection of Zycus on 30 Jun 2026 after a POC that landed us the 'best platform' verdict, and both sides are now in Order Form, MSA and SOW contracting. The deal is won-in-principle; the only real question is whether SOW scoping, legal redlines and InfoSec/Workday integration can complete by the 31 Jul close, which is tight given the SOW workshop is not yet booked.
north_star_verdict.math: 25 days to close (31 Jul, today 6 Jul). Created 27 May 2025, ~405 days old, Shortlisted stage. Selection confirmed 30 Jun ('you have chosen to partner with Zycus'). Order Form plus revised MSA (amended to the UK entity) sent 2 Jul. SOW scoping workshop proposed 1 Jul but not yet booked (BH flagged 1 Jul that 'Monday will be a rush for us and we need prep'). Legal (Catherine Wilson) and InfoSec/IT (Michael White) not yet engaged this cycle. A 2-3 week SOW + legal + InfoSec cycle starting this week lands ~24-27 Jul, preserving 31 Jul ONLY if the workshop books this week and the Order Form is countersigned by ~14 Jul. Any slip in booking the workshop pushes signature into August. Pace required: lock SOW dates by 10 Jul, countersign Order Form/MSA by 14 Jul, legal + InfoSec in parallel by 24 Jul.
evidence: Selection confirmed: Claire Hudson to Elaine Rymill, 30 Jun 2026 email, 'Super excited that you have chosen to partner with Zycus... collating the pack to send across, including the SOW template and revised MSA'.

POC won on merit: Next_Step__c reads 'POC was successful - best Platform'; the AP POC hit a ~97 percent automatic invoice match with line items auto-extracted (Nishita, Drop In Session AP, 9 Jun 2026).
Contracting underway: Order Form plus updated MSA amended to the UK entity sent to Elaine Rymill on 2 Jul 2026 (SF task, Clari email).
Forecast raised Best Case to Commit by Anthony Gray on 1 Jul 2026 (field history), consistent with a confirmed selection.

Timing is the sole open risk: BH replied 1 Jul 'we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end, Monday will be a rush for us', so the SOW scoping workshop is not yet booked with 25 days to close.

Economic buyer sign-off not yet evidenced in writing: Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and a CFO (Elizabeth) were referenced for a Boston executive validation meeting (internal note 16 Jun 2026) but neither has appeared on a recorded call.

metrics.narrative: The operational pain is well evidenced but the value case remains qualitative, not quantified by Bright Horizons. In the POC the AP team saw a ~97 percent automatic invoice match ('the success rate of all the invoices which you have sent was roughly 97 percent', Nishita, Drop In AP call, 9 Jun 2026) with line items and three-way match handled automatically, and nursery managers valued the basket and catalog efficiency (Lauren Ross, 2 Jun 2026). Zycus asserted up to 30 percent better spend management (Anthony Gray, 26 Mar 2026 supplier presentation), but BH has never attached its own numbers (hours saved, headcount, savings target) to the business case. Quantifying ROI with finance (Callum Waring, Finance Business Partner, or Martin Grover, Finance Manager) before the £251k economic-buyer sign-off is the move.
sources: Avoma Drop In Session AP 9 Jun 2026

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped but not yet engaged in writing. Joanne Urso (Vice President, Procurement, SFDC contact role) is the budget authority for the £251k and was referenced alongside a CFO (Elizabeth) for a Boston executive validation meeting (internal note, 16 Jun 2026: 'Boston Executive Meetings: Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and Elizabeth (CFO)'). Julie Sheppard (Head of Business Transformation) is the named Executive Sponsor. Neither Joanne, the CFO, nor Julie has appeared on any recorded Avoma call; the commercial and pricing conversations have run entirely through Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement) and Elaine Rymill (Procurement Manager). The evidence that budget authority exists is strong (selection confirmed, forecast raised to Commit), but written EB sign-off on the £251k is the single unclosed gate. The move is for Andrew to convert the Boston executive discussion into written proceed-to-contract authority from Joanne Urso.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Joanne Urso, VP Procurement; Julie Sheppard, Exec Sponsor)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and were tested through a structured, multi-team POC rather than a slide review. BH ran a formal tender against six core requirement areas Anthony Gray read back on 26 Mar 2026: intelligent supplier management, AI-driven contract lifecycle, seamless purchasing and cataloging, goods receipt automation, reporting, and supplier portal self-service ('you wanna drive smarter, risk aware sourcing... embed control and speed into contracting through automation'). The POC then validated ease-of-use (nursery managers navigating baskets, favorites and GenAI keyword-free search unaided, Lauren Ross 2 Jun 2026), AP automation (97 percent invoice match, 9 Jun 2026), and end-to-end sourcing-to-contract flow with Workday as the source of truth (Nishita, 2 Jun 2026). Ease of use for non-procurement nursery staff and picture-rich catalogs were named as decisive ('Andrew said it's just so important to have those pictures on', Lauren Ross, 2 Jun 2026). Zycus met the criteria and was chosen 'best Platform'.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer ran a disciplined enterprise tender with a wide committee, and the call sequence maps the process: supplier presentation/demo (26 Mar 2026, ~115 min, Andrew Duckworth, Elaine Rymill, Catherine Wilson legal, finance and procurement coordinators), pricing/model workshop (13 May), sandbox POC kickoff (18 May) with team-by-team drop-in sessions across nurseries, AP, legal, sourcing and IT (26 May to 9 Jun), then down-selection to selection on 30 Jun. Elaine Rymill owns the tender day-to-day; Andrew Duckworth drives the decision. The approval chain steps up to Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and a CFO for executive validation (Boston meeting, 16 Jun) before contract execution. The process is now in contracting: Order Form and revised MSA out 2 Jul, SOW scoping workshop being scheduled, with contract redlining and signing targeted for July per Next_Step__c.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are active but several tracks are not yet open with 25 days to close. Zycus sent the Order Form plus a revised MSA amended to the UK entity ('our UK entity is fine from a credit perspective') to Elaine Rymill on 2 Jul 2026, and a two-hour SOW scoping workshop was proposed 1 Jul but is not yet booked (BH: 'we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end', 1 Jul). Legal review sits with Catherine Wilson (Legal Counsel), a named tender stakeholder from 26 Mar who is not yet re-engaged this cycle. InfoSec and the Workday integration scope belong to Michael White (Head of Software Engineering) and are not yet started; Zycus confirmed it has Workday integration experience but no prebuilt connector (Nishita, 2 Jun 2026) and will integrate with BH's existing DocuSign signature license. The SOW is the choke point and the signature predictor here: signature will not land until the SOW is agreed, so booking that workshop this week is the forcing action. No PO is expected as a gate for a UK invoice-direct entity.

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is concrete and owner-attributed. Nursery managers struggle with the current clunky system: baskets 'never worked or it's never been a smooth process' and product search forces exact keywords (Lauren Ross, 2 Jun 2026), and Lauren asked for order-cutoff-time visibility she does not have today ('having that message of... the cutoff for this supplier is 3PM'). AP (Stuart Lamont, Transactional Services Manager, and Bev Patrick, AP Manager) carry manual invoice matching that Zycus automated to ~97 percent in the POC. The pain owners are clear: nursery operations (Lauren Ross), AP/transactional (Stuart Lamont, Bev Patrick), and procurement process ownership (Andrew Duckworth, Elaine Rymill). Workday is the mandated single source of truth everything must integrate to (Elaine, 2 Jun 2026).

champion.narrative: There are two aligned advocates. Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker contact role) leads the tender, drove the commercials and vendor selection, and personally pushed the picture-rich catalog requirement. Elaine Rymill (Procurement Manager) is the daily operational champion coordinating every session, the sandbox rollout, and the Order Form/MSA/SOW logistics, in near-daily email contact through 1-2 Jul 2026. Their advocacy is proven: BH chose to partner with Zycus on 30 Jun 2026. The remaining development is champion access to power: neither has yet pulled Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) or the CFO into a direct budget conversation, so written EB sign-off still depends on Andrew escalating internally.

competition.narrative: Zycus has won the selection. Bright Horizons down-selected from a shortlist that included GEP and Coupa and chose Zycus after a successful POC ('POC was successful - best Platform'; selection confirmed 30 Jun 2026), so GEP and Coupa are now out-competed and declined. Proactis is the incumbent P2P provider being displaced (contract expiry Mar 2027). The only live competitive threat now is Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal driven by timing: if SOW, legal and InfoSec slip past the contracting window, BH could defer or extend Proactis rather than lose to a rival. The wedge is a phased P2P-first rollout aligned to BH's finance constraints, with Merlin Intake AI as the Phase 2 value unlock; the win move is to remove timing drift by booking the SOW workshop this week.

deal_movement.summary: Deal moved from active POC to confirmed selection and contracting in the last two weeks. BH confirmed 'you have chosen to partner with Zycus' on 30 Jun; Zycus sent the Order Form and revised UK-entity MSA on 2 Jul; forecast was raised Best Case to Commit on 1 Jul. The SOW scoping workshop is proposed but not yet booked, and EB written sign-off is still open.

items.change: Selection confirmed: BH chose to partner with Zycus after the POC ('best Platform')
Order Form plus revised MSA (amended to the UK entity) sent to Elaine Rymill
SOW scoping workshop (two-hour agenda) proposed to BH; BH flagged they need prep before booking
Forecast raised Best Case to Commit by Anthony Gray
Amount settled at £250,945.49 after commercial refinement from a £400k peak (18 May)
Final commercial/pricing pack and clarifications exchanged with Andrew Duckworth and Elaine Rymill

competitive_position.summary: Zycus is the selected vendor as of 30 Jun 2026, having won the POC ('best platform') over the GEP and Coupa shortlist. GEP and Coupa are now declined (out-competed at selection, 30 Jun 2026). Proactis is the incumbent P2P provider being displaced (contract expiry Mar 2027). The single strongest CURRENT threat is Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal driven by timing: if SOW, legal and InfoSec slip past the contracting window, BH could defer signature and extend Proactis. CLM specialists (iCertis, SIREN Labs) and Checker were early-2025 peripheral mentions, now dormant.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal
competitors.quote: BH replied 1 Jul 'we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end, Monday will be a rush'; SOW workshop not yet booked with 25 days to close.
competitors.how_we_win: Anchor a mutual close plan to the July signature window and the Mar 2027 Proactis cutover; book the SOW workshop this week so timing cannot cause drift.

competitors.name: Proactis
competitors.quote: Proactis is the clunky incumbent P2P setup being replaced; lacks AI-driven intake and supplier risk automation, catalog management outdated.
competitors.how_we_win: Displace fully with the phased P2P-first rollout and the POC-proven three-way match and catalog UX; keep the Mar 2027 expiry as the forcing function.

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: Shortlisted alongside Zycus and Coupa for the vendor selection; Zycus was chosen to partner on 30 Jun 2026.
competitors.how_we_win: Selection already won; keep the POC differentiation (ease of use, GenAI, integration breadth) front of mind through contracting.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Shortlisted alongside Zycus and GEP; not selected, Zycus chosen 30 Jun 2026.
competitors.how_we_win: Selection already won; ensure commercials stay within the validated envelope so price cannot reopen the field.

competitors.name: Checker (P2P alternative)
competitors.quote: Alternatives mentioned include systems like Proactis and Checker for P2P.
competitors.how_we_win: No longer in the frame post-selection; retain as history only.

competitors.name: iCertis (CLM)
competitors.quote: specialized CLM providers such as iCertis and SIREN Labs.
competitors.how_we_win: Zycus contracting/redlining covers CLM in-suite; no standalone CLM needed.

competitors.name: SIREN Labs (CLM)
competitors.quote: specialized CLM providers such as iCertis and SIREN Labs.
competitors.how_we_win: In-suite contract management removes the standalone CLM case.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus met the tender's six core requirement areas and won on ease-of-use and AP automation; the fit is confirmed. The remaining exposure is delivery-side: the Workday integration is experience-based not prebuilt, and one nursery order-cutoff feature is a build-on-request item.

items.criterion: Ease of use for non-procurement nursery staff (baskets, favorites, keyword-free GenAI search)
items.quote: We'll love that. That's so much easier than last time... you can type in pretty much any word, and it will come up with the most close products.

items.criterion: AP invoice automation and three-way match
items.quote: the success rate of all the invoices which you have sent was roughly 97 percent... all the line items are extracted automatically.

items.criterion: Workday integration as the single source of truth
items.quote: We don't have a prebuilt integrator, but we have experience of integration with Workday, so we know exactly which points to reach.
currently that functionality is not there. We can build on it if it's like a deal breaker... we can put it across voting (AI council).

items.requirement: Provide order-cutoff-time visibility (e.g. 'order by 2PM for next-day delivery' banner) on catalog/punch-out pages
items.quote: having that message... the cutoff for this supplier is 3PM, to have that kind of visual.

items.requirement: Confirm the Workday integration approach and points of connection for the S2P rollout
items.quote: Workday is our one source of truth, so everything will have to talk to Workday.

items.deliverable: Send the SOW template, revised UK-entity MSA and Order Form pack to Elaine Rymill
items.grounding_quote: collating the pack to send across, including the SOW template and revised MSA... Please find attached the Order Form along with the updated MSA.

items.deliverable: Integrate Zycus with BH's existing DocuSign signature license and confirm the Workday integration points
items.grounding_quote: Bright Horizons uses a particular signature platform, and we will be integrating with that... we have experience of integration with Workday, so we know exactly which points to reach.

items.deliverable: Provide dates and prep to book the two-hour SOW scoping workshop
items.grounding_quote: we do need to do a little engagement and prep this end. Monday will be a rush for us.

items.deliverable: Countersign the Order Form and revised UK-entity MSA
items.grounding_quote: Please find attached the Order Form along with the updated MSA... Could you please send this to your team.

Catherine Wilson (Legal) and Michael White (IT) named as tender stakeholders but not yet re-engaged this contracting cycle.

best_practice_check.summary: A won-in-principle deal whose only enemy is the calendar: lock the SOW workshop, convert exec validation to written sign-off, and open legal/InfoSec in parallel this week.
flags: Book the SOW scoping workshop with Elaine Rymill this week; the SOW is the signature predictor and it is the one gate that decides whether 31 Jul holds.
Convert the Boston exec validation into written proceed-to-contract sign-off from Joanne Urso (VP Procurement); it is the only open commercial gate on a Commit forecast.

champion_strength.summary: Champion strength is strong and multi-person: Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker) leads the tender and drove the selection of Zycus, while Elaine Rymill (Procurement Manager) is the daily operational champion coordinating the sandbox, SOW and MSA logistics through 1-2 Jul 2026. Their advocacy is proven by the 30 Jun 2026 decision to partner with Zycus. The one remaining development is champion access to power: neither has yet pulled Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) or the CFO into a direct budget conversation.
champion_strength.champion: Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement) with Elaine Rymill (Procurement Manager)
champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; SF email 30 Jun 2026; SF activities 1-2 Jul 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Merlin Intake is the sole AI module in a four-product S2P scope, positioned as a Phase 2 value unlock rather than a deal headline. The agentic/autonomous-sourcing story was shown (Nishita flagged the agentic framework and autonomous sourcing agents Andrew asked about, 18 May 2026) but is not yet the centre of the buyer's business case; the win was driven by ease-of-use and AP automation, not AI.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS is 5.0 / Moderate: Merlin Intake is the only AI SKU in a four-module S2P deal, a faint AI signal. The calls corroborate genuine but secondary AI interest, Andrew asked to see the autonomous sourcing agents and the GenAI RFP-builder landed well with the new procurement manager, but the buyer bought on usability and automation, not on AI. Not over- or under-rated versus the field value.

items.detail: SOW scoping workshop not yet booked with 25 days to close; BH said they need prep, so signature could slip into August.
Economic buyer Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and the CFO have not engaged or signed off in writing on the £251k.
Workday integration is experience-based with no prebuilt connector, and InfoSec/IT (Michael White) is not yet engaged this cycle.
Amount settled at £251k after a refinement down from a £400k peak (18 May), consistent with a phased P2P-first scope; watch for further envelope pressure at signature.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and warm: near-daily two-way email through 1-2 Jul 2026, a confirmed selection, and active contracting. The only cooling risk is the unbooked SOW workshop stalling momentum.
items: Order Form and MSA out 2 Jul 2026; buyer actively coordinating the SOW workshop.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: We are the selected vendor at Bright Horizons after a POC that earned the 'best Platform' verdict, with Andrew Duckworth and Elaine Rymill racing us toward a 31 Jul signature; the only things between us and the win are booking the SOW scoping workshop and turning the Boston exec validation into written sign-off from VP Procurement Joanne Urso.

win_position.text: Score is high because selection is confirmed, not forecast: Claire Hudson's 30 Jun email captures BH saying 'you have chosen to partner with Zycus' after the POC won on 'best Platform', held below a pure ceiling only by the still-open SOW and EB sign-off.
The win was earned on evaluated merit, ease-of-use for nursery staff and a 97 percent AP invoice match in the POC (Nishita, 9 Jun 2026), over a shortlist of GEP and Coupa that are now declined.

Risk to the number: economic buyer Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) and the CFO were only referenced for a Boston validation meeting (internal note, 16 Jun) and have never engaged, so written £251k sign-off is unproven.

Risk to the number: the SOW scoping workshop is not yet booked with 25 days to close (BH said 1 Jul 'Monday will be a rush, we need prep'), so timing, not a rival, is what could push signature into August.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum is strong: selection confirmed 30 Jun, Order Form and revised UK-entity MSA sent 2 Jul, and forecast raised Best Case to Commit on 1 Jul (field history).
Commercial and legal topics are live, near-daily email between Anthony Gray, Claire Hudson and Elaine Rymill on the proposal, MSA and SOW through 1-2 Jul 2026.
The one drag on momentum is that the SOW workshop and legal/InfoSec tracks have not opened, so the contracting relay could serialize past 31 Jul.

customer_commitment.text: Commitment is explicit: BH invested 17-19 stakeholders across multi-hour workshops and a two-week sandbox POC, then put the selection in writing on 30 Jun 2026.
The buyer is doing the contracting work with us, requesting the SOW workshop agenda and reviewing the Order Form and revised MSA amended to their UK entity.
Commitment is not yet ratified at the top: no economic-buyer or CFO signature, and BH asked for prep time before committing SOW dates.

deal_risk.text: Close-date risk is the primary threat: a 2-3 week SOW plus legal plus InfoSec cycle only fits 31 Jul if the workshop books this week and the Order Form is countersigned by ~14 Jul.
Do Nothing / Proactis short-term renewal is the live fallback, not a rival win, if timing slips BH can extend the incumbent (expiry Mar 2027) and defer.
The amount came down from a £400k peak (18 May) to £251k, consistent with a defensive phased P2P-first scope, so watch for further envelope pressure at signature.

close_plan_concretizing.evidence: Contract redlining and signing targeted for July per Next_Step__c; Order Form and MSA out 2 Jul, but SOW workshop not yet booked.

executive_alignment.evidence: EB Joanne Urso and CFO referenced for Boston validation but never engaged or signed off in writing.

scope_change.from: Full-suite S2P at a ~£400k envelope (peak 18 May 2026)
scope_change.to: Phased P2P-first S2P rollout at ~£251k (settled 30 Jun 2026)
scope_change.detail: Amount refined down from a £400k peak (Anthony Gray, 18 May) through £189k and £250k to £250,945.49, consistent with a phased P2P-first implementation aligned to BH's finance constraints rather than a big-bang buy. The four modules (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, Merlin Intake) remain, but the reduced envelope signals a cost-conscious, phased posture to watch at signature.

customer_preference.evidence: 'POC was successful - best Platform' (Next_Step__c) and 'you have chosen to partner with Zycus' (Claire Hudson to Elaine Rymill, 30 Jun 2026); chosen over GEP and Coupa.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain and a POC-demonstrated 97 percent AP invoice match, but BH has not quantified its own ROI (hours, headcount, savings); Zycus asserted up to 30 percent spend-management gains (26 Mar 2026).
